Introduction to Bayesian Inference and Modelling (2023/24) Welcome Content Structure People & Contact Details

Introduction to Bayesian Inference and Modelling (2023/24) Welcome UCL Social Data Institute (SODA) is hosting this 4-day course on Bayesian inference. The aim is to introduce academics and professional data analysts to the basics of Bayesian inference using RStudio and Stan. The atmosphere of the course will be friendly and supportive, with the goal to teach the absolute basics of Bayesian inference in Stan for academics and professionals alike from diverse backgrounds ranging from industry to research fields such as population health, social sciences, disaster risk reduction, and many more. Content The course will cover the following topics: Date Downloadables Topics Not Applicable Installation [Data] Pre-learning materials 10/06/2023 [Slides] [Data] Introduction to Bayesian Inference 11/06/2023 [Slides] [Data] Bayesian Generalised Linear Models 12/06/2023 [Slides] [Data] Bayesian Hierarchical Regression Models 13/06/2023 [Slides] [Data] Spatial Bayesian Risk Modelling By the end of the workshop, the participants should: Acquire the foundation and advanced knowledge on key principles of statistical modelling within a Bayesian framework; Be able to perform inferential statistics on spatial and non-spatial data to carry out hypothesis testing for evidence-based research using the diverse types of regression-based models from a Bayesian framework; Be able to perform spatial risk prediction for areal data as well as quantify levels of uncertainty using exceedance probabilities; Acquire new programming language skills such as Stan (interfaced with RStudio). Structure Throughout the course, each day will consist of a lecture, a walkthrough demonstration and computer seminar session. Here is the information on the programme structure: Times Format 11:00am-12:00pm (1h) Lecture 12:00pm-01:00pm (1h) Lunch Break 01:00pm-02:00pm (1h) Live Walkthrough and Demonstration 02:00pm-02:15pm (0h15mins) Short Break 02:15pm-03:45pm (1h30mins) Computer Practical Session 03:45pm-04:00pm (0h15mins) Wrap-up All sessions are delivered in-person at the Charles Bell House G11 Seminar Room 4, 43–45 Foley Street, W1W 7TY. Please bring your own laptops with you to the computer practicals. People & Contact Details Name Email Anwar Musah (Instructor, UCL Geography) a.musah@ucl.ac.uk Moira Hague (Institute Manager, UCL SODA) m.hague@ucl.ac.uk Tom O’Grady (Deputy Director, UCL SODA) t.o’grady@ucl.ac.uk James Cheshire (Director, UCL SODA) james.cheshire@ucl.ac.uk
1 Getting started 1.1 What is Stan? 1.2 Installation of R and RStudio (Window and MAC)

1 Getting started 1.1 What is Stan? Stan is an interface for several statistical software packages (e.g., RStudio, Python, Julia, Stata, and MATLAB) which allows the user to perform state-of-the-art statistical modelling within a Bayesian framework. The focus of this workshop will be solely on Stan and RStudio. We will show you how one can develop and compile Stan scripts for Bayesian inference through RStudio to perform basic parameter estimation, as well as a wide range of regression-based techniques starting with the simplest univariable linear models and its different families to the more advanced multivariable spatial risk models. Before all that, let us install the appropriate software. The next sections will guide you through the installation process. 1.2 Installation of R and RStudio (Window and MAC) R, or RStudio is a statistical software programming package that allows the user to carry out different types of statistical analysis. The famous icon on the left is the version for R (Base), and the one on the right is the version for RStudio. Both software packages are the same. The only difference is that RStudio is attractive, intuitive, and more importantly, it is user-friendly than Base R. So, we will be using RStudio throughout the Bayesian course. In order for RStudio to work, you must first install R (Base) before RStudio. You can follow the steps and use the table below to download the correct version for your operating system (i.e., Windows or MAC). Steps: Download the file (i.e., .exe or .pkg) for R (Base) in accordance with your operating system from the links provided in the table below. Next, install it by clicking on the downloaded file (i.e., .exe or .pkg). Now, we can download the file (i.e., .exe or .dmg) for RStudio in accordance with your operating system from the links provided in the table below. You can install it by clicking on the downloaded file (i.e., .exe or .dmg). OS User type R (Base) RStudio Desktop Windows R-4.3.1-win.exe RStudio-2023.06.0-421.exe MAC R-4.3.1.pkg RStudio-2023.06.0-421.dmg Make sure to install the latest version of R and RStudio that is appropriate for your operating system. As of June 27, 2023, the latest version of RStudio (i.e., Windows 10/11 or macOS 11+) is version 2023.06.0-421. For base R, it is version 4.3.1. 1.2.1 Additional installation steps (MAC users only) For macOS users, after you have completed the installation for R and RStudio, you are required to install the following applications: XQuartz and XCode XQuartz Version 2.8.5 Some functions in R and Stan require some of the libraries from XQuartz to function properly on your MAC. Steps: Download the latest version of XQuartz (XQuartz-2.8.5.pkg) by clicking on this LINK Double-click the downloaded XQuartz-2.8.5.pkg file and follow the steps to complete the installation. XCode Version 14.3.1 (14E300c) Some functions in R and Stan require some of the libraries and developer tools from XCode to function properly on your MAC. Steps: Go to the App Store application and get the XCode app downloaded by clicking on this LINK. Once it is downloaded, you can click on the “OPEN” button to verify it’s been downloaded. A window will prompt you to complete installation. Lastly, and for safe measures - we going to run this through our Terminal. You can open the Terminal program by going to the Applications > Utilities folder and select the “Terminal” application In the terminal, type the following code xcode-select --install. If you get the following message shown, then it means that the XCode program has installed properly. Otherwise, the terminal will proceed to install XCode tool remotely. xcode-select: error: command line tools are already installed, use "Software Update" in System Settings to install updates 1.2.2 Additional installation steps (Windows users only) Rtools 4.3 For Windows users, after you have completed the installation for R and RStudio, you are required to install the Rtools package as it contains some libraries and developer tools for the smooth functioning of Stan. Steps: Download the latest version of Rtools43 by clicking on this LINK to initiate the download of the Rtools43 installer. Double-click the downloaded rtools43-5550-5548.exe file and follow the steps to complete the installation.
